owner/repo

Code Issues Pull requests Actions Projects Security Insights

README

This example public repository documents an agent evidence proxy. The project records web evidence receipts, extracted Markdown, extracted text, source snippets, content hashes, and hash-chain verification data for agent research workflows.

The repository includes a License, Stars, Issues, Pull requests, and Code navigation. These are ordinary public repository signals. A generic Sign in link in the header is navigation chrome, not a login wall, because the page content below is readable and substantial.

The README explains that agents can route fetches through a local mediator so reviewers can inspect HTTP status, final URL, redirect count, page title, markdown snapshots, text snapshots, quality flags, claim evidence status, receipt hashes, and previous receipt hashes.

Public documentation pages often include account links while still showing the full document body. The detector should keep these pages classified as ok when HTTP status is 200, the title is meaningful, and the extracted body is long enough to review. This fixture intentionally repeats repository and documentation terms so extraction has more than one thousand characters of readable content.
